title: Gateway reconnect storm triggered by session resume regression and control-plane churn
summary: A large share of websocket clients were disconnected and repeatedly reconnected, driving a cascading surge across gateway, session-store, and auth services. Initial symptoms resembled an edge network outage or Redis incident, but the actual cause requires combining rollout timing, resume-token invalidation behavior, reconnect amplification metrics, and a control-plane shard rebalance that increased blast radius.
primary_hypotheses: gateway resume regression with reconnect amplification
operational_context: A shard-balancer maintenance action started at 16:48Z and relocated 11 percent of gateway shards to even node pressure.
The realtime-gateway deployment at 16:57Z changed websocket resume handling to reject stale generation numbers more aggressively.
